steps.title: Find the passage focus
steps.content: The text says Ruth Asawa was dedicated to more than creating art: she wanted to bring art to children in San Francisco. The examples that follow are arts education programs she helped create.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported summaries
steps.content: Choice A says she inspired other artists, which is not the main claim. Choice B invents a local-versus-national comparison. Choice D says she left sculpting to teach, which the passage does not state.

steps.title: Summarize the central idea
steps.content: The remaining choice correctly states that Asawa's interest in art education led her to create programs for San Francisco students. The answer is C.
